name: What kind of compensation can I get for a back injury from lifting heavy boxes in the warehouse?
description: Medical, 66% lost wages, disability pay via workers' comp.[1]

mainEntity.name: What kind of compensation can I get for a back injury from lifting heavy boxes in the warehouse?

acceptedAnswer.text: Medical, 66% lost wages, disability pay via workers' comp.[1]
acceptedAnswer.description: **Workers' comp covers medical expenses, lost wages (typically 66% of average weekly wage), and permanent disability benefits if applicable.** Pain/suffering isn't covered, but third-party suits might add that.[1][3] Key factors: prove injury arose from employment; maximums vary by state (Utah caps at 312 weeks). Document doctor visits and work restrictions; negotiate settlements carefully. Values depend on injury severity - severe cases reach six figures.[3]
